name: archetypes/evidence
title: Evidence
description: A pull-quote with a named source, corroborated by exactly one piece of proof — a stat, chart, or figure that lands on the first advance.
useCases: backing a results slide with a customer's own words
pairing a testimonial with the number that makes it credible

attribution.description: A real name and role; anonymous praise proves nothing.

proof.description: Exactly one stat, chart, or figure inside ls-quote__evidence that corroborates the words.

motion.notes: The quote enters with the slide; the proof (ls-quote__evidence) carries ls-reveal with data-step="1" so the number lands after the words.

icons.guidance: No icons; the attribution and the proof carry the credibility.

useWhen: a claim needs a human voice and a number together
closing an argument with corroborated testimony
avoidWhen: no proof exists for the quote — use components/quote alone rather than inventing a stat
the numbers are the story and the voice is decoration — use archetypes/big-stat

alternatives.when: the quote stands alone without proof

attributes.name: data-ls-archetype
attributes.description: Marks the slide for contract linting and deck scorecards.

usage: Trim the quote to the sentence with the verdict in it; 30 words is a ceiling, not a target.
The proof must measure what the quote claims — a mismatched stat reads as spin.
Attribute to a person with a role, not a logo.
